questions.text: Which symbol is used for assignment in WJEC pseudocode?
questions.explanation: WJEC pseudocode uses ← for assignment, e.g. x ← 5. The = sign is reserved for comparisons in some pseudocode conventions.

questions.text: What shape is used to represent a decision in a flowchart?
questions.explanation: A diamond represents a decision (yes/no question) in a flowchart. Rectangles are for processes, ovals for start/stop, and parallelograms for input/output.

questions.text: A linear search is performed on a sorted list of 1000 items. In the worst case, how many comparisons are needed?
questions.explanation: Linear search checks each element one by one. In the worst case (item is last or not in list), all n elements must be checked — 1000 comparisons.

questions.text: What is the main pre-condition for binary search to work correctly?

options.text: The list must contain only integers
The list must have an odd number of elements
The list must be stored in an array

questions.explanation: Binary search works by halving the search space based on whether the target is greater or less than the middle element. This only works if the list is sorted.

questions.text: What is the maximum number of comparisons needed to find a value in a sorted list of 128 items using binary search?
questions.explanation: Binary search requires at most log₂(n) comparisons. log₂(128) = 7, since 2⁷ = 128.

questions.text: Which sorting algorithm works by repeatedly finding the minimum element from the unsorted portion and placing it at the front?
questions.explanation: Selection sort finds the smallest element in the unsorted portion and swaps it to its correct position, then repeats for the remaining unsorted part.

questions.text: Trace bubble sort on the list [4, 2, 7, 1]. What is the list after the FIRST complete pass?
questions.explanation: Pass 1: compare 4,2 → swap [2,4,7,1]; compare 4,7 → no swap [2,4,7,1]; compare 7,1 → swap [2,4,1,7]. After first pass: [2, 4, 1, 7].

questions.text: What is the difference between validation and verification?

options.text: Validation checks data is correct; verification checks it is reasonable
Validation checks data is reasonable; verification checks it matches the original source
Validation is done by the user; verification is done by the computer

questions.explanation: Validation checks data is reasonable and meets rules (e.g. range check). Verification checks data was entered correctly — it matches the original source (e.g. double entry).

questions.text: Which type of validation check would detect that a user entered their age as 200?
questions.explanation: A range check verifies the value is within acceptable limits (e.g. 0 to 120). An age of 200 would fail a range check.

options.text: A variable that can be accessed from anywhere in the program
A variable that stores the current location in the program
A variable declared inside a subroutine, only accessible within it
A variable stored on the local hard drive

questions.explanation: A local variable is declared inside a subroutine and can only be accessed within that subroutine. It is created when the subroutine is called and destroyed when it ends.

questions.text: What does SUBSTRING("Computing", 1, 4) return in WJEC pseudocode?
questions.explanation: SUBSTRING(str, start, length) returns 'length' characters starting at position 'start' (1-indexed). SUBSTRING("Computing", 1, 4) starts at position 1 and takes 4 characters: "Comp".

questions.text: Which construct would you use to repeat a block of code a fixed number of times?
questions.explanation: A FOR loop is count-controlled — it repeats a fixed number of times. A WHILE loop is condition-controlled — it repeats until a condition becomes false.

questions.text: What is a rogue/sentinel value used for?

options.text: To indicate an error in the program
To signal the end of data input in a condition-controlled loop
To store the largest value found so far

questions.explanation: A sentinel (rogue) value is a special input value that signals the end of data. For example, a user enters -1 to indicate they have finished entering numbers.

questions.text: What is one advantage of using subroutines in a program?

options.text: Code can be reused without rewriting it
It removes the need for comments

questions.explanation: Subroutines promote code reuse — write a subroutine once and call it as many times as needed. This also makes code easier to maintain and test.

questions.text: In insertion sort, what happens to the element being inserted?

options.text: It is swapped with its neighbour repeatedly until in position
It is compared with the minimum and swapped to the front
Larger elements to its left are shifted right to make room, then it is placed
It is removed from the list and added to a new sorted list

questions.explanation: Insertion sort takes each element (the 'key'), then shifts all elements in the sorted portion that are larger than it one position right, before inserting the key into the gap.
